questions: Why does the CLI hardcode isHelixTagged: true on every comment?
What should the CLI send instead?
Does the CLI need to store or display user identity?

answers.answer: post.ts:32 sends { content: message, isHelixTagged: true } unconditionally. This was likely a shortcut since the CLI was initially designed for sandbox agents that should always be associated with Helix. With the server-side identity fix, this is wrong because external CLI users would also have their comments Helix-tagged.
evidence: helix-cli/src/comments/post.ts:31-33 — body: { content: message, isHelixTagged: true }

answers.answer: The CLI should send only { content: message } without isHelixTagged. The server will determine isHelixTagged based on auth identity: for Helix agents (isHelixAgent JWT claim), the server forces isHelixTagged=true and isAgentAuthored=true. For external CLI users (API key auth), the server uses the client-provided value (defaulting to false). This is the simplest and most correct approach — the server is the source of truth for identity.
evidence: comment-controller.ts:65-66 — isHelixTagged defaults to false if not provided
comment-controller.ts:77 — server already has logic to force isHelixTagged for agent comments

answers.answer: No changes needed to identity storage. The CLI config stores { apiKey, url } which is sufficient. Identity is resolved server-side: API keys map to createdByUserId (the logged-in user), and inspection JWTs map to the JWT subject. The CLI doesn't need to know or display who it is — the server handles all attribution.
evidence: helix-cli/src/lib/config.ts:5-8 — HxConfig = { apiKey: string; url: string }

statement: The CLI fix is minimal: remove the hardcoded isHelixTagged: true from post.ts:32 and send only { content: message }. The server determines whether to set isHelixTagged based on the auth identity (Helix agent vs external user). No other CLI changes are needed.
